meaning: vt.涂香油于(尸体),施以防腐剂,铭记于心,使不朽
examples: "First of all," interposed Laigle, "I demand permission to embalm Blondeau in a few phrases of deeply felt eulogium.
Myrrh was a spice used to embalm the dead.
We must embalm the master's wise opinions.
Maduro announced Thursday that the government would embalm Chavez's body and put it on permanent display, a decision that touched off strong passions on both sides.
The methods used to embalm the head also matched techniques in use at the time of his death, said the scientists in a report published by the British Medical Journal (BMJ).
